name: Mistral la Plateforme API
what_it_does: Provides hosted access to Mistral's commercial model lineup (Mistral Large, Mistral Small, Codestral) with function calling, JSON mode, and EU data residency via a REST API distinct from self-hosted open-weight deployments.
use_cases: Building tool-calling agent pipelines with Mistral Large's native function calling for structured tool dispatch
Generating guaranteed-valid JSON outputs for data extraction agents using JSON mode with schema enforcement
EU-regulated applications requiring data processing and inference within European data centers
Code generation and completion agents using Codestral, Mistral's code-specialized model
Cost-sensitive production deployments using Mistral Small for high-volume agentic tasks where frontier models are unnecessary
not_for: Workloads requiring self-hosted or on-premises deployment — use open-weight Mistral models via vLLM or NIM for that
Multimodal inputs such as image understanding — Mistral la Plateforme is text-only
Agents needing real-time web search or built-in retrieval without custom tool integration
best_when: You need EU data residency, reliable function calling, and a balance of capability and cost on a fully managed commercial API.
avoid_when: You need maximum frontier reasoning capability — Mistral Large trails GPT-4o and Claude 3.5 Sonnet on complex multi-step reasoning benchmarks.

auth.notes: API key as Bearer token in Authorization header. Keys created per workspace in la Plateforme dashboard. No scope restriction per key.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Experimental tier with free access to certain models at reduced rate limits
pricing.paid_tiers: Per million input/output tokens, tiered by model size. Mistral Large costs more than Mistral Small.
pricing.notes: Experimental free tier available for testing. Premier tier for production with higher rate limits and SLA. No minimum spend required.

performance.rate_limits: Experimental: 1 req/s. Premier: higher limits per model — documented in console.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Clear error messages with HTTP status codes and JSON body. Function call validation errors surface cleanly. Context length errors include token counts.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Stateless inference. Same prompt and parameters produce consistent outputs per model version.
known_agent_gotchas: Function calling format differs slightly from OpenAI — tool_choice parameter syntax and parallel tool call behavior require testing before assuming OpenAI parity
JSON mode requires explicitly instructing the model to output JSON in the system prompt — the parameter alone does not guarantee structure without prompt engineering
Codestral requires a separate API key from the standard la Plateforme key in some account configurations
Model versioning uses dated snapshots (e.g., mistral-large-2407) — pinning to 'mistral-large-latest' can cause silent model swaps that change agent behavior
EU data residency endpoint is not the default — agents must explicitly configure the EU endpoint if residency compliance is required

security_score_components.security_notes: API keys lack scope granularity. GDPR-compliant with EU data residency option. DPA available. No IP restriction on free tier.
